title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Mysterious type of static electricity has a hidden culprit
items.description: Find explains how volcanic ash plumes spark lightning and why dust in grain silos can explode

items.title: Mitochondria packaged in blood cell membranes improve disease symptoms in mice
items.description: Tiny capsules can deliver healthy organelles into animals with Parkinson’s-like and other mitochondria-linked disorders

items.title: Computer science’s ‘Nobel Prize’ goes quantum
items.description: Charles Bennett and Gilles Brassard share this year’s $1 million A.M. Turing Award for work on quantum information

items.title: Scientific conferences can be a bore. Can jokes liven them up?
items.description: <cite>Science</cite> speaks with ecologist Stefano Mammola about the power of humor to enhance science communication

items.title: White House lifts hold on NIH research spending
items.description: Director Jay Bhattacharya tells House lawmakers that grantmaking, hiring are on track

items.title: Colliding currents can target the deep brain without surgery
items.description: Human studies show promise of stimulation approach for epilepsy and other diseases

items.title: This hot pink cricket is no mutant
items.description: Arota festae’s unusual hue may help it blend in with immature plants

items.title: Research on fruit flies and other ‘model’ organisms may be declining
items.description: Analysis of published papers on eight widely studied species suggests work on them is fading, but not everyone is worried

items.title: Can potatoes grow on the Moon?
items.description: Experiments show the crop can survive in lunar soil—with a lot of help

items.title: Ancient DNA reshapes the story of early Pacific migrations
items.description: Neolithic seafarers who settled Palau were already a blend of Asian and Papuan peoples
